content: Exercise prescription in CR: initial assessment includes symptom-limited exercise test to determine functional capacity, ischemic threshold, arrhythmia risk, BP response. FITT principle: Frequency (3-5 days/week aerobic, 2-3 days/week resistance), Intensity (aerobic: 40-80% heart rate reserve or RPE 11-14 on Borg scale; use heart rate at ischemic threshold minus 10 bpm if present; resistance: 30-40% 1RM upper body, 50-60% lower body initially, progress to 60-80%), Time (20-60 minutes continuous or accumulated aerobic; 1-3 sets of 10-15 repetitions resistance), Type (rhythmic large muscle groups for aerobic—walking, cycling, rowing; major muscle groups for resistance). Warm-up and cool-down essential (5-10 minutes each). Monitoring: telemetry initially for high-risk patients, BP before/during/after exercise, symptoms (angina, dyspnea, dizziness), RPE. Contraindications to exercise: unstable angina, decompensated HF, severe AS, uncontrolled arrhythmias, acute PE, myocarditis/pericarditis, resting SBP >200 or DBP >110. Progression based on tolerance, goals, comorbidities.
